text: **Anna:** Hello!
**Anna:** Yesterday was the most {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい} day.
**Anna:** I want to tell my {friend|友人} back home about it.
**Anna:** So I am writing her a letter.
**Anna:** Dear Penelope
**Anna:** Life in Washington, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** is interesting.
**Anna:** I see something new every day, like yesterday.
**Anna:** Yesterday started like a {usual|普通の/通常の} workday.
**Anna:** I said yesterday started like a usual workday.
**Anna:** I was at work and I wanted a {break|休憩/中断|/breɪk/|Let's take a short break.}
**Anna:** So I walked and {walked|歩いた} and walked.
**Anna:** And walked.
**Anna:** Then I saw something.
**Anna:** It was a {festival|祭り/祭典|/ˈfestəvəl/|The music festival was fun.}
**Anna:** A big festival.
**Anna:** It's the Smithsonian {Folk|民族の/民俗の} Life Festival.
**Anna:** Yes, it was the Smithsonian Folk Life Festival.
**Anna:** Every year, the festival {shows|見せる/展示する} different cultures.
**Anna:** This year, one of the cultures was the {Basque|バスク地方の} culture.
**Anna:** There was dancing and food and {games|ゲーム/遊び}.
**Anna:** I am dancing a {traditional|伝統的な} mask dance!
**Anna:** At the festival, I danced a traditional Basque dance.
**Anna:** Maybe July, maybe in two months, maybe three months, and that's it.
**Anna:** They are cooking traditional Basque food.
**Anna:** They cooked a lot of traditional Basque food.
**Anna:** Nice shot.
**Anna:** They are playing a game.
**Anna:** It's a kind of {handball|ハンドボール}.
**Anna:** What do they {call|呼ぶ/名付ける|/kɔːl/|Can you call my name?} it?
**Anna:** They {call|呼ぶ/名付ける|/kɔːl/|They call it a game.} it {pilota|バスク地方の球技|/pɪˈloʊtə/|Pilota is a traditional sport.}
**Anna:** Oh the
**Anna:** They {played|遊ぶ/演じる|/pleɪd/|Kids played in the park.} a game with their {hands|手|/hændz/|Raise your hands.} and a small {ball|ボール|/bɔːl/|Throw the ball.}
**Anna:** It's a kind of {handball|ハンドボール|/ˈhændbɔːl/|Handball is a team sport.}, but this game is called {pilota|バスク地方の球技|/pɪˈloʊtə/|Pilota requires skill.}
**Anna:** Nice.
**Anna:** Oh, this is {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|What a beautiful day.}
**Anna:** This is {traditional|伝統的な|/trəˈdɪʃənəl/|Traditional music is important.} Basque art.
**Anna:** They are making a {traditional|伝統的な|/trəˈdɪʃənəl/|Traditional cooking takes time.} fast {chip|チップ/切れ端|/tʃɪp/|Potato chips are tasty.}
**Anna:** This {festival|祭り|/ˈfestɪvəl/|Music festival is exciting.} is a lot of fun.
**Anna:** The whole day was a lot of fun.
**Anna:** Who said that?
**Anna:** I want to {write|書く|/raɪt/|Write a letter to friend.} my friends and tell them about my day.
**Anna:** So I wanted to tell you about my day.
**Anna:** Please, my friend, come {visit|訪問する|/ˈvɪzɪt/|Visit my hometown.} Washington, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** soon.
**Anna:** There is a lot to do.
**Anna:** Until next time.
